skill.name: ppt-orchestra-skill
skill.description: Plan and orchestrate multi-slide PowerPoint creation from scratch. Use before generating a full deck with subagents: classify each slide type, enforce visual variety, set typography/spacing rules, and run text-based QA to catch content issues.
skill.summary: Orchestrates professional PowerPoint deck planning with slide classification, design rules, and quality assurance.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: All 31 static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The scanner incorrectly flagged hex color codes (e.g., '22223b', '4a4e69') as weak cryptographic algorithms, markdown code block delimiters as shell command execution, and layout documentation text as system reconnaissance. The skill is a legitimate PowerPoint planning and orchestration guide. Shell commands referenced (node compile.js, python -m markitdown, grep) are standard local development tools with no security risk. No prompt injection or malicious patterns were found.

content.value_statement: Creating consistent, visually varied presentations is difficult when working slide by slide. This skill plans layout, typography, and visual rules before generation, and provides quality checks to ensure professional results.
actual_capabilities: Classify slides into 5 standard page types: Cover, Table of Contents, Section Divider, Content, and Summary
Enforce consistent typography and spacing rules across all slides in a deck
Generate Node.js compile scripts to assemble individual slide modules into a complete PPTX file
Run text-based QA to verify content completeness and detect leftover placeholder text
Recommend font pairings and color themes for professional presentation design
Define layout options such as two-column, grid, and half-bleed image arrangements
limitations: Does not generate PPTX files directly - provides planning and orchestration only
Requires pptxgenjs and markitdown to be installed separately by the user
Content QA is text-based only and cannot verify visual rendering or layout accuracy
Limited to the 5 predefined slide page types for consistent structure

use_cases.title: Marketing pitch deck planning
use_cases.description: Plan a branded pitch deck with consistent slide types, typography rules, and visual variety before generating the final presentation.

use_cases.title: Consulting presentation QA
use_cases.description: Run quality assurance checks on generated client presentations to catch placeholder text, missing content, and formatting inconsistencies.

use_cases.title: Educational lecture slides
use_cases.description: Structure lecture presentations with clear section dividers, content pages with data visualization, and summary slides for key takeaways.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a simple 5-slide deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a 5-slide presentation for a product launch. Classify each slide as one of the 5 page types and specify the layout, typography, and visual elements for each slide.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a 10-slide sales deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a 10-slide sales presentation with these sections: Cover, Table of Contents, Problem, Solution, Features, Comparison, Data, Testimonials, Summary, and Contact. For each slide, specify the page type, layout option, font pairing, and color theme.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a conference presentation with data slides
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a 15-slide conference presentation. Include Cover, Table of Contents, 3 Section Dividers, 8 Content pages (mix of data visualization, comparison, and timeline types), and a Closing page. Define a custom color theme, enforce typography rules, and specify QA checkpoints for each content slide.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced - large presentation with mixed content types

prompt_templates.title: Full orchestration with QA loop
prompt_templates.prompt: Orchestrate a complete presentation workflow: classify all slides by type, generate individual slide modules, create the compile script, run the QA verification loop using markitdown text extraction, check for placeholder text with grep, fix identified issues, and re-verify affected slides. Report all issues found and fixes applied.
prompt_templates.scenario: Expert - end-to-end workflow with quality assurance

output_examples.input: Plan a 5-slide product launch deck with a modern design theme.
output_examples.output: Slide plan generated: Cover (full-bleed background, 44pt title), Table of Contents (3 sections with icon rows), Content - Features (two-column layout with data callouts), Content - Timeline (process flow with 4 steps), Closing (key takeaways and contact info). Typography: Georgia headers, Calibri body. Theme colors applied. Compile script structure provided.

output_examples.input: Run QA on my generated presentation to check for placeholder text and missing content.
output_examples.output: QA report: 3 issues found. (1) Slide 4 contains 'lorem ipsum' placeholder text in body paragraph. (2) Slide 7 is missing the source citation for the chart data. (3) Slide 2 has center-aligned body text instead of left-aligned. Recommended fixes provided for each issue. Re-verification required after applying fixes.

best_practices: Classify every slide into one of the 5 page types before generating content to ensure consistent structure
Run the QA verification loop at least once with a fix-and-recheck cycle before declaring success
Commit fully to either a simple or complex layout style across the entire presentation - avoid mixing styles
anti_patterns: Centering body text instead of left-aligning paragraphs and lists
Repeating the same layout pattern across all slides without visual variety
Declaring the presentation complete without completing at least one fix-and-verify cycle

faq.question: What are the 5 slide page types this skill uses?
faq.answer: The 5 page types are: Cover Page (title and tone setting), Table of Contents (navigation), Section Divider (transitions between parts), Content Page (text, mixed media, data visualization, comparison, timeline, or image showcase), and Summary/Closing Page (wrap-up and action items).

faq.question: Does this skill generate the actual PPTX file?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides planning and orchestration. It generates instructions, slide classifications, and a Node.js compile script that uses pptxgenjs to create the final PPTX file. You must install pptxgenjs separately.

faq.question: What tools do I need installed to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need pptxgenjs for creating presentations (npm install -g pptxgenjs) and markitdown for text extraction QA (pip install markitdown[pptx]). Both are referenced in the skill instructions.

faq.question: How does the QA process work?
faq.answer: After generating slides, extract text using markitdown, review for missing content and typos, check for leftover placeholder text using grep, list all issues found, fix them, and re-verify affected slides. Repeat until no new issues appear.

faq.question: Can I customize the color theme and fonts?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill provides a default theme structure and font pairing table. You can define custom colors in the theme object and choose any font pairing from the provided table or use your own.

faq.question: How many slides can this skill handle?
faq.answer: The compile script defaults to 12 slides but you can adjust the loop count. The skill works best for presentations with 5-20 slides. For larger decks, consider breaking them into sections with separate compilation.
